shortTerm: Expected excellent short-term outcomes with apixaban initiation. Stroke risk immediately reduced from 6.7% to 0.6% annually (91% reduction). First 3 months critical for medication adherence and monitoring bleeding events.
longTerm: Projected 5-year stroke prevention >85% with sustained anticoagulation. Quality of life expected to remain stable with proper management. Annual reassessment of CHA2DS2-VASc score and bleeding risk warranted.
riskFactors: Advanced age (65 years) increases bleeding risk 1.5-fold
Hypertension (controlled) - minor elevation in hemorrhagic stroke risk
Type 2 diabetes - vascular complications may affect outcomes
Prior stroke history - recurrence rate 10-15% in first year despite therapy
protectiveFactors: Normal renal function (eGFR >60) allows standard dosing
No history of major bleeding or intracranial hemorrhage
Strong medication adherence demonstrated in diabetes management
Regular healthcare engagement and access to specialty care
motivationFactors: Patient highly motivated by stroke prevention after recent TIA. Family support strong. Understands risks/benefits of anticoagulation.
previousTreatmentResponse: Good response to antihypertensives and metformin. No adverse reactions to cardiovascular medications in past 10 years.
insightLevel: Excellent understanding of atrial fibrillation, stroke risk, and anticoagulation rationale. Able to articulate treatment goals.
mortality: 5-year mortality estimated 15-20% (primarily cardiovascular causes). Anticoagulation reduces stroke-related mortality by 60-70%.
functionalStatus: Independent in all ADLs. NYHA Class I. No functional limitations related to cardiac condition.
findings: Permanent atrial fibrillation confirmed on 12-lead ECG. Echocardiogram shows preserved LVEF (55%). No valvular abnormalities.
assessment: 65-year-old male with permanent AF, CHA2DS2-VASc score 5, HAS-BLED score 2. High stroke risk, moderate bleeding risk. Optimal candidate for oral anticoagulation.
plan: Initiate apixaban 5mg BID. Follow-up in 2 weeks for bleeding assessment. CBC and renal function at 1 month. Patient education on bleeding precautions.

recommendations.recommendation: Continue apixaban indefinitely unless contraindications develop
Annual reassessment of CHA2DS2-VASc and HAS-BLED scores
Immediate evaluation for any unexplained bleeding or bruising

notes: Patient counseled on importance of medication adherence and bleeding precautions. Will monitor INR if switched to warfarin in future.
